textByHash.e81818bdd0333a96b8c847262d3fb5309ce03152: Have you seen that dog? It's a nasty piece of work. Even I don't like going near it.
textByHash.18fac61882f827bfb69cbb99f4800c24ef6f2e7d: I'm not meant to talk to anyone outside the family. On pain of death.
textByHash.596d80111adde74ecf9678c97ea73b1af51aef83: What? I'm no gossip; I've got nothing to say about it.
textByHash.28dee856a61092fcfc652e642a0748a5c1ce19e6: Dang, that's too bad for you. Isn't it a bit of conflict of interest, considering you were part of the investigation that proved she was guilty?
textByHash.a6e0b49c603f98fb8b81d5de77c38cf5e2079f5a: I fed the dog and went to the inn in town. When I got back the guards were all over the place.
textByHash.d455dbf4bed3ede6997b3adcdda67d141552dbe8: Didn't see any of the family. I was outdoors most of the evening, tending to things, then went to town.
textByHash.0bf98802f23750f02930cee3357150bed44c85de: That dagger? I think Lord Sinclair kept it in the library. It was only decorative, so never paid much attention to it.
textByHash.5d41326ba46011b6b1cae97534307e038f8019fb: I'm not sure; it could be Anna's or David's.
textByHash.cbeb80b688e7ea9107c904870bc0a824f29efa04: The poison salesman came around the mansion and practically everyone bought some.
textByHash.4f1ff99ad24b1cd4bba9d7faa727363dbd610e13: Each of the Sinclair kids did. They all had chores they were meant to do. I had a chat with that salesman. Whoo, you any idea how strong that stuff was? Just a small amount would be enough to kill a cow.
textByHash.e215d7509fa0b6728f587023911b7f99b414a088: Yup, that's what the poison salesman said. I had a chat with him at the pub.
textByHash.6f1406277d75293dc5123858e71fd6a7a7662c14: Each of the Sinclair kids did; they all had chores they were meant to do. I had a chat with that salesman. Whoo-boy, you any idea how strong that stuff was? Just a small amount would be enough to kill a cow.
textByHash.459312bfaa9ad60eb93e155139beea04c9d882be: The guards told me not to talk to anyone.
textByHash.afd96c06dba7439bf057d774964f20935c0fd3ee: Honestly? I think it was Carol.
textByHash.93ab1545fad3a1e0bc6374255dde0d375ed8a6b4: I saw her in a huge argument with Lord Sinclair in the library the other day. It was something to do with stolen books. She definitely seemed upset enough to have done it afterwards.
textByHash.0d659beef30e3124ded167443fd7640bd648745d: I was in town at the Inn. When I got back the house was swarming with guards who told me what had happened. Sorry.
textByHash.3db579f77d7df87cd65bd4e5632bd9e94946e096: No, I don't remember hearing anything like that.
textByHash.0ad510f0542d07b0ae33a6787efda80762fe8de1: I hear him bark all the time. It's one of his favorite things to do. I can't say I did the night of the murder though as I wasn't close enough to hear either way.
textByHash.4505dfe39b1ed5ee0229788415f8536e6221537f: Well, I know David said that he was going to do something about the spiders' nest that's between the two servants' quarters upstairs.
textByHash.2de295df472bfbb4eed776c959303731bb607d35: He made a big deal about it to Mary the Maid, calling her useless and incompetent. I felt quite sorry for her actually. You'd really have to ask him though.
textByHash.18959360704439440518ef5628185039ce76b4f6: Thank you for all your help in solving the murder.
